verses:
nare narottama dhanya, granthākāra agragaṇya,
agaṇya puṇyera ekādhāra
sādhane sādhaka⋅śeṣṭha, dayāte ati gariṣṭha
iṣṭa prati bhakti camatkāra

candrikā pañcama sāra, tina maṇi sārātsāra,
guruśiṣya saṁvāda⋅paṭala
tribhuvane anupama, ‘prārthanā’ granthera nāma,
‘hāṭa⋅pattana’ madhura kevala

racilā asaŋkhya pada, haiyā bhāve gadagada,
kavitvera sampada se saba
je śune jebā paḍe, jebā tāhā gāna kare,
sei jāne padera gaurava

sadā sādhu⋅mukhe śuni’, śrī⋅cetanya āsi puni,
narottama⋅rūpe janamilā
narottama guṇādhāra, vallabhe karahe pāra,
jalete bhāsāo punaḥ śilā
en_translation:
Among men, Śrīla Narottama Dāsa Ṭhākura is glorious. He is the foremost author and a unique reservoir of countless virtues. In spiritual practice, he is the best of all practitioners, and in compassion, he is supreme. His devotion to his chosen Deity is wonderful.

His book 'Prema-bhakti-candrikā' is the fifth essence, and his 'Prārthanā' is the essence of essences of the three jewels—a conversation between Guru and disciple. In the three worlds, his 'Prārthanā' is incomparable, and 'Hāṭa-pattana' is exceedingly sweet.

He composed countless verses while overwhelmed with ecstatic emotion; all of them are the wealth of poetry. Whoever hears, reads, or sings them knows the true glory of these verses.

I always hear from the mouths of the saintly that Lord Caitanya came again and was born in the form of Narottama. O Narottama, abode of all good qualities! O Master, please deliver me and make the stone float on water once again.
